Package de.uka.ilkd.key.settings

Interface Settings

All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractPropertiesSettings, AbstractSettings, ChoiceSettings, ColorSettings, ExtensionSettings, FeatureSettings, GeneralSettings, KeyStrokeSettings, LemmaGeneratorSettings, NewSMTTranslationSettings, ProofCachingSettings, ProofDependentSMTSettings, ProofIndependentSMTSettings, SlicingSettings, StrategySettings, TermLabelSettings, TestGenerationSettings, ViewSettings
public interface Settings
This interface is implemented by classes that are used to store settings for different proposes (like active heuristics, which LDTs to use etc.)

    Description
    void
    addPropertyChangeListener(@NonNull PropertyChangeListener listener)
    Register a new listener which is triggered for changes on properties.

    Register a new listener which is triggered for changes on the specified property.

  • Method Details

    • readSettings

      @Deprecated void readSettings(Properties props)
      Deprecated.
      Deprecated in favour of readSettings(Configuration)
      gets a Properties object and has to perform the necessary steps in order to change this object in a way that it represents the stored settings

    • writeSettings

      @Deprecated void writeSettings(Properties props)
      Deprecated.
      Deprecated in favour of writeSettings(Configuration)
      The settings to store are written to the given Properties object.
      Parameters:
      props - the Properties object where to write the settings as (key, value) pair

    • readSettings

      void readSettings(@NonNull Configuration props)
      This method transfers the given configuration information into the local states. The setter methods are used so PropertyChangeEvent should be triggered accordingly to the new state.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null references to a configuration object. The state of this object shall not be changed by the implementations.

    • writeSettings

      void writeSettings(@NonNull Configuration props)
      The internal state is stored in the given configuration object. The stored information must be sufficient to restore the local state.

      The internal state shall not be changed by the implementations.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null reference to a configration object, which state is modified accordingly to the local internal state.

    • addPropertyChangeListener

      void addPropertyChangeListener(@NonNull PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Register a new listener which is triggered for changes on properties.
      Parameters:
      listener - a non-null reference
      See Also:
      • PropertyChangeSupport.addPropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener)

    • removePropertyChangeListener

      void removePropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Removes the given listener.
      Parameters:
      listener - a non-null reference
      See Also:
      • PropertyChangeSupport.removePropertyChangeListener(PropertyChangeListener)

    • addPropertyChangeListener

      void addPropertyChangeListener(@NonNull String propertyName, @NonNull PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Register a new listener which is triggered for changes on the specified property.
      Parameters:
      propertyName - the name for identification of the property
      listener - the listener to be added
      See Also:
      • PropertyChangeSupport.addPropertyChangeListener(String, PropertyChangeListener)

    • removePropertyChangeListener

      void removePropertyChangeListener(@NonNull String propertyName, @NonNull PropertyChangeListener listener)
      Removes the given listener from being triggered by changes of the specified property.
      Parameters:
      propertyName - the name for identification of the property
      listener - the listener to be removed
      See Also:
      • PropertyChangeSupport.removePropertyChangeListener(String, PropertyChangeListener)
